name: Not medically necessary
strategy: Cite clinical guidelines (MCG, InterQual, ACR Appropriateness Criteria) and peer-reviewed studies demonstrating medical necessity. Reference the patient's specific clinical presentation, failed conservative treatments, and progressive functional decline. Include objective clinical measurements and validated outcome scores.
required_evidence: Clinical guidelines supporting the procedure (MCG criteria, specialty society guidelines)
Peer-reviewed studies demonstrating efficacy for this clinical scenario
Documentation of failed conservative treatments with dates and durations
Objective clinical measurements (ROM, pain scales, functional scores)
Payer's own published coverage policy language supporting authorization
Provider attestation of medical necessity
escalation_path: If first-level appeal denied: request peer-to-peer review with payer medical director. If peer-to-peer fails: file second-level appeal citing additional clinical evidence. Final step: external independent review organization (IRO) review.

name: Invalid procedure code / modifier combination
strategy: Review and correct the CPT/HCPCS code and modifier combination. Verify the code matches the documented procedure. If code was correct, provide detailed operative/procedure notes showing why the code is appropriate. Include coding references (CPT Assistant, CMS NCCI edits) supporting the code selection.
required_evidence: Corrected claim with appropriate CPT/HCPCS codes and modifiers
Operative or procedure notes supporting code selection
CPT Assistant or CMS coding references validating the code
NCCI edit review showing no bundling conflicts
Modifier documentation (if applicable)
escalation_path: Resubmit corrected claim. If still denied: submit formal appeal with coding specialist documentation. Escalate to payer coding review department.

name: No precertification / authorization on file
strategy: Provide proof that prior authorization was obtained before the service date. Include authorization reference numbers, dates, and confirmation correspondence. If authorization was not obtained, argue retroactive authorization based on emergent/urgent medical necessity or demonstrate that the service was not on the payer's PA-required list at the time.
required_evidence: Authorization reference number and date of approval
Written or electronic confirmation of authorization
Fax confirmation logs or portal screenshots showing submission
Date-of-service documentation proving authorization preceded service
If retroactive: documentation of emergent medical necessity
Payer's PA-required procedure list at time of service
escalation_path: Submit retroactive authorization request with urgency documentation. If denied: file formal appeal with authorization evidence. Escalate to payer's grievance and appeals department.

name: Claim/service lacks information needed for adjudication
strategy: Identify exactly what information is missing from the original submission and provide it. Common missing items include: clinical notes, lab results, imaging reports, demographic information, or referring provider data. Resubmit with complete documentation package.
required_evidence: Complete clinical documentation package
All supporting lab results and diagnostic reports
Patient demographic information verification
Referring/ordering provider information
Itemized list of previously missing items now included
Cover letter specifying what was added
escalation_path: Resubmit with complete information. If denied again: call payer to identify specific missing items. File formal appeal with comprehensive documentation package.

name: Benefit maximum reached
strategy: Review the patient's plan documents to verify benefit limits. If the limit is incorrect, provide evidence of the actual plan terms. If the limit is correct, file a medical exception request arguing that continued treatment is medically necessary to prevent harm or hospitalization. Cite cost-avoidance data.
required_evidence: Patient's specific plan benefit summary showing actual limits
Medical exception request form (payer-specific)
Clinical documentation showing medical necessity beyond benefit maximum
Cost-avoidance analysis (continued treatment vs. hospitalization/ER)
Provider letter explaining clinical consequences of stopping treatment
Evidence of treatment progress and remaining treatment plan
escalation_path: File medical exception request with clinical justification. If denied: appeal to plan administrator with cost-avoidance argument. Request external review if available under state law.

name: Non-covered service / charge
strategy: Review plan coverage documents to verify the service is truly excluded. If coverage exists, provide plan language showing the service is covered. If excluded, argue for coverage exception based on medical necessity — that no covered alternative exists and denial would cause patient harm. Check for parity laws (mental health) or state mandates.
required_evidence: Patient's plan coverage documents / Summary of Benefits
Evidence of benefit coverage for the service category
Medical necessity documentation if requesting exception
Letter explaining no adequate covered alternatives exist
Applicable state or federal mandates requiring coverage
Mental health parity analysis (if behavioral health service)
escalation_path: File formal coverage exception request. If denied: appeal citing plan language, state mandates, or parity laws. Escalate to state insurance commissioner if payer violates coverage requirements.

name: Service not covered - member responsibility
strategy: Verify the service is truly a member exclusion vs. a payer processing error. Review the plan's evidence of coverage document. If the service should be covered, provide plan language. If truly excluded, file a medical exception arguing unique clinical circumstances that make this service the only viable option.
required_evidence: Plan Evidence of Coverage document
Summary of Benefits and Coverage
Medical exception request with clinical justification
Provider letter explaining unique clinical circumstances
Documentation showing no covered alternative treatments exist
Patient consent and acknowledgment
escalation_path: File medical exception request. If denied: appeal through plan's formal grievance process. Consider external review or state insurance department complaint.

name: Payment adjusted - diagnosis not covered
strategy: Review the diagnosis codes submitted and ensure they accurately reflect the clinical documentation. If codes are correct, argue that the diagnosis warrants the service based on clinical evidence and guidelines. Provide updated clinical notes with more specific diagnosis coding if applicable.
required_evidence: Updated or corrected diagnosis codes (highest specificity)
Clinical documentation supporting the diagnosis
Clinical guidelines linking diagnosis to requested service
Peer-reviewed literature supporting treatment for this diagnosis
Provider narrative explaining clinical rationale
ICD-10 coding references supporting code selection
escalation_path: Resubmit with corrected/updated diagnosis codes. If denied: formal appeal with clinical rationale. Request peer-to-peer review to discuss clinical correlation.

name: Payment adjusted / reduced
strategy: Challenge the payment adjustment with clinical documentation demonstrating the full scope and complexity of the service provided. Include detailed procedure notes, time documentation, and evidence supporting the billed level of service. Reference fee schedule and contract terms.
required_evidence: Detailed operative/procedure notes documenting full scope of service
Time documentation (if time-based billing)
Clinical complexity documentation
Contracted fee schedule terms
Comparison of billed vs. paid amount with justification
Medical decision-making documentation supporting service level
escalation_path: Submit formal payment dispute with supporting documentation. If denied: appeal through provider dispute resolution process. Escalate to state insurance department if contract terms violated.

name: Other insurance primary / coordination of benefits
strategy: Investigate coordination of benefits (COB) to determine correct primary payer. Provide updated COB information and proof of other coverage status. If this payer is truly primary, provide evidence (e.g., birthday rule, employment status, Medicare Secondary Payer rules).
required_evidence: Updated coordination of benefits information
Other insurance EOB or denial showing non-coverage
Evidence of correct primary payer determination
Birthday rule documentation (for dependent coverage)
Employment status verification (for employer-sponsored plans)
Medicare Secondary Payer questionnaire (if applicable)
escalation_path: Submit updated COB information and reprocess request. If denied: contact both payers to resolve COB dispute. File formal COB investigation request with primary payer determination.
